session_summary: Marcus spun up a fresh, independent audit session for the just-landed W2 LRT-differential material (@sec-lrt, Propositions L1-L4) in derivation.qmd, instructing the auditor to treat every claim as suspect and re-derive the mathematics before reading the proofs. The single human turn is a fully specified verification protocol: derivation-audit, blind re-derivation, reproduction of the CAS/Monte-Carlo verification chain, an audit of the prior session's own cross-model grading, and an end-to-end H1 over-claim check, all under a strict scope fence. The human's role here is process architect: he designs an adversarial, independence-maximizing verification ritual rather than asking for new results.

coded_turns.quote:
You had NO part in
  producing the material you are auditing — treat every claim as suspect until verified against
  artifacts you open yourself.
coded_turns.note: Designs an independent adversarial audit: re-derive L1-L4 before reading proofs, reproduce CAS/MC chain, audit the grading itself.
